page: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 48 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8462Y+ processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system features 960.0 GB of system memory, providing 20.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, delivering a combined 320 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, meaning external storage solutions must be attached.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier capabilities in several areas. The server achieves a Passmark CPU score of 85,426.66 and an extended instructions score of 117,918.35, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. This is supported by hardware-level support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 7,649.82 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the system processes 111.99 million Redis SET operations per second and demonstrates strong LLM inference speeds, processing 10,308.98 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model.

In terms of resource density, the server represents a high-end hardware class designed for massive parallel processing. While the shared CPU allocation model presents a potential performance consistency tradeoff under full multi-tenant load, the dedicated physical core count and massive GPU memory pool offset this for accelerator-bound tasks. The lack of built-in local storage requires users to factor in the cost and latency of network-attached storage.

This server is engineered for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, including LLM training and inference, deep learning, and complex scientific simulations. It is also highly capable of handling memory-intensive database operations, high-throughput cryptography, and parallel data decompression tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8462Y+ processor and 960.0 GB of RAM. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with a total of 320 GB VRAM, though it lacks local storage. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 85,426.66, and memory bandwidth reaching 7,649.82 GB/sec for small block reads. It also demonstrates high efficiency in data processing, executing 111.99 million Redis SET operations per second and 10,308.98 tokens per second for small LLM prompt processing. While the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage represent operational tradeoffs, the massive GPU and memory resources make this server highly effective for deep learning, large-scale AI inference, and parallel scientific computing.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 Intel Xeon cores, 960 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 320 GB VRAM, designed for high-end AI and parallel computing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-960GB-4xH100 features 48 Intel Xeon cores, 960 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA H100 GPUs for high-performance AI and data workloads.
tagline: A high-density accelerator platform combining four Hopper GPUs with massive system memory for parallel computing and artificial intelligence workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 320 GB total VRAM
48 physical Intel Xeon 8462Y+ cores with AMX and AVX-512 support
960 GB of system memory delivering high-bandwidth read performance
Top-tier performance in database operations and LLM prompt processing
No local storage included, requiring external storage configuration
Best suited for deep learning, AI inference, and scientific simulations
